market_overview.headline: Market consolidates near highs with 43% of stocks in Full Bull mode despite cautious positioning ahead of heavy earnings week.
market_overview.detail: SPY and QQQ both in Full Bull formation but SPY showing bearish cross risk with compressed EMA spreads. BTC remains in Bear Rally mode, creating divergence from traditional risk assets.

reversal_candidates.headline: Bear Rally Reversal Candidates - Early Stage Trend Changes

items.note: Cybersecurity leader showing strong momentum with 8W EMA inches from bullish cross

items.note: Asset management giant showing technical repair as markets consolidate at highs

momentum_themes.headline: Memory & Semiconductor Renaissance Leading Tech Recovery
momentum_themes.detail: Memory sector exploding with SNDK up 68% vs 21W EMA, driven by AI demand sustainability through 2030. Electronic technology sector showing broad acceleration with storage and networking equipment leading the charge.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominance vs Tech Services Weakness

strongest.note: Perfect bullish structure with Iran tensions supporting oil infrastructure plays
Industrial strength continues with broad-based momentum across manufacturing
Logistics and shipping benefiting from supply chain normalization

weakest.note: Biotech and medical device companies under pressure amid regulatory concerns
Software and cloud names still working through valuation reset despite recent bounces
Telecom sector pressured by competitive dynamics and infrastructure costs

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from growth/tech services into energy and industrials as geopolitical tensions support commodity-exposed sectors. Memory semiconductors providing bridge between old tech weakness and new cycle acceleration.

items.text: SPY showing bearish cross risk with compressed EMA spreads - watch for breakdown below 691
BTC divergence from equity markets could signal correlation snap ahead
High volume selling in energy pullbacks (CNQ, PSX) suggests institutional distribution
Heavy earnings week ahead with rangebound pre-announcement positioning

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Memory sector rally dominated on persistent AI demand outlook through 2030 per Melius Research. Iran tensions supported energy infrastructure while markets remained cautious ahead of heavy earnings week with mixed Microsoft-OpenAI deal news providing tech sector nuance.

items.text: Micron and Sandisk surge on Melius Research report calling for sustained memory demand through decade-end
Iran oil embargo and Hormuz Strait tensions boosting energy infrastructure and alternative supply chains
Microsoft-OpenAI deal restructuring creating winners and mixed signals for broader AI investment cycle
Wall Street positioning defensively ahead of major earnings releases with rangebound trading
Geopolitical risk premium supporting energy minerals while pressuring traditional defensive sectors
